title: Catwalk Gait Analysis Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a5eai","text":"Motor and gait impairments are important outcome measures for many disease phenotypes. The following is a protocol for assessing motor and gait behavior using the Catwalk XT gait analysis platform at the University of Alabama at Birmingham in the Neuroscience Behavioral Assessment Core.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"367sf","text":"Use of this protocol needs approval by user's Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) or equivalent ethics committee.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"1gfht","text":"You will need a test mouse whose behavior is not included in the experiment to calibrate detection settings","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Bring animals into testing room for habituation (at least 30 minutes prior to start of experiment)
Darker lights increase the sensitivity of the platform
Make note of light settings so that you can remain consistent across runs
Open the Catwalk XT Version 10.6 program
Camera should be 35 cm away for mice or 70 cm for rats
Define the walkway – drag the box to take up the whole width and length of the catwalk that is within the camera view

Calibrate walkway – place a calibration paper down on the walkway and drag the square in the program the same size Tell the program how big the square is based on the known size of your calibration paper
Indicate where the file needs to be saved
Choose mouse or rat based on what type of animal is being tested
Input the groups that you have (i.e. control, experimental, sham, etc)
Criteria: Minimum run time = 0.5 seconds Maximum run time = 5.0 seconds Runs = 3 Maximum variation = 60%
Add the number of trials equivalent to the number of animals being tested
Optional: add the trials to defined groups to separate out experimental groups
Place a test mouse inside the corridor
Then adjust the intensity threshold to clear out noise Look at prints to ensure that only the feet are registering (and no other body parts)
Discard runs noncompliant with run criteria
Discard incomplete prints at the start and end of a run
Optional: toggle on/off sound feedback as desired
Use the “UN ->” and “<- UN” to navigate through unclassified prints
Click and drag over UN to identify the unclassified prints
If UN is not a print, select the print and classify as junk
Make sure all date and all parameters are selected on the left
Scroll through the run to visualize the prints
Illustrates both the print view and the temporal view of each run
Lists the statistic broken down by parameter for each run
Shows the data broken down by group (if groups were used)
Automatically saves in the export folder
Run for a total of 3 compliant runs for each animal
creator: Ashley Adamson
